title: Pull big layers from gitlab (>1GiB)
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Cannot download layers of more than 1GiB from gitlab as it seems to cut layers in different http calls.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Get a gitlab instance

2. pull an image with a layer of, say, 1.4Gib

3. download will always fail at 1.0Gib

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$> podman pull instance/image:latest
Trying to pull instance/image:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 2cba8f394bae done
Copying blob a53e1a350ebd done
Copying blob b6b0e5bba19c done
Copying blob e59c28769f97 done
Copying blob dbba69284b27 done
Copying blob 3cd53964ee48 done
Copying blob f7596f8333b0 done
Copying blob e71d156c1f99 done
Copying blob 5c7083529f82 done
Copying blob dd7310ba3488 done
Copying blob d44c1954521c [==========================>-----------] 1.0GiB / 1.4GiB
Copying blob 9f8a3fa66ed2 done
Copying blob d95c372a50ba done
Error: writing blob: storing blob to file "/var/tmp/storage3555584973/11": happened during read: unexpected EOF
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

No errors.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Pulling with the docker binary is fine.

Gitlab registry seems to this intentionnaly, in the log we can see that the layer is detected to it's correct size. The http request seems to have behaved correctly according to logs, there is no error. But the size is of 1GiB.
Maybe podman need to retry?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.1.0
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```

Bug is existing also on podman 3.4.7

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes, seems debian experimental provides the latest podman

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Note that is is maybe a weird behaviour of gitlab. If proven so, I can open a bug there. But as docker works fines with it...

comments.body: A good sample is this image: docker.io/reactnativecommunity/react-native-android:latest
@qdel, thanks for reaching out.  Are you running behind a proxy?  If so, could you try without it?

Hi,

I am not using a proxy on the client side.

I have 2 gitlab instances. One with a reverse proxy, one without.

Both experience the same issue.
Does wireshark indicate whether the connection to the registry dropped?

I am not a pro of wireshark and i will try to create a testing environment for it.

But indeed, in the instance using my reverse proxy, I encouter this error:

```
upstream prematurely closed connection while reading upstream, client: XX.XX.XX.XX, server: XXXXX, request: "GET /v2/dockerbases/debian-qt-builders/blobs/sha256:18c6995dfe2f865881316f5c6ddcb26b54acf76ff9bd1521b9e803be6f1c53ed HTTP/1.1", upstream: "http://192.168.125.14:8881/v2/dockerbases/debian-qt-builders/blobs/sha256:18c6995dfe2f865881316f5c6ddcb26b54acf76ff9bd1521b9e803be6f1c53ed", host: "XXXXXX"
```

When requesting directly my registry in http using following command, the process go to the end.
```
$> podman pull --tls-verify=false 192.168.125.14:8881/dockerbases/debian-qt-builders:latest
```

I am searching if there is configuration in my reverse proxy which can create the hassle.
Not that in the instance of gitlab without reverse proxy, ssl is enabled by gitlab config. I think it host a nginx itself for ssl-isation. (so, there is a reverse proxy also in this case)

Maybe not a podman issue!

Thanks for checking, @qdel!

That somehow confirms my initial suspicion. Such `EOF` errors are usually caused by proxies. I wished I could help debug the proxy but I am a terrible sys admin.

Hi,

Indeed, this was the case. Note that this post helped me:
https://trac.nginx.org/nginx/ticket/1472

Switching configuration of my proxy with `proxy_max_temp_file_size 0;` correct this issue.
I will provide the info to gitlab in case of...

Note that I am still quite surprised docker does not meet this error. maybe I should check the differences in the http request of both clients. Maybe docker puts a magic header!

Thanks anyway for your help!

Thank you for sharing, @qdel!

Maybe nginx special cases if the client is Docker?  Just a random guess.
Docker does have some per-layer retry logic that seems to reuse data from previous aborted pull attempts.
